relative_path: LAB CLEANING/Cleaning day 20250528/Lab data cleaning day.docx
full_text:
Special activities for 28052025

Some external hard drives are missing - we don’t know where they are. Let’s find them all and put the information here: Storage units taks and comments in the subtable “Hard disks”.

In the instruction document below we provided a file structure example, try to organise your project in this way.

General activities

Remove as much unnecessary data as possible from your computer, hard drive, P-drive, L-drive, cpu1-data/ (see the next guidelines).

Update in the Wiki inventory if you are using currently a new hard drive or computer: https://wiki.helsinki.fi/xwiki/bin/view/FL/Farkkila%20Lab/IT%20Infrastructure/Inventory/

Re-organize your data in a more efficient way if possible.

Compress all raw and intermediate data from the Cold storage disks, even if it's not yours.

Detect unused files owned by others in your workstation or laptop, CSC. Backup those files or delete them, consult this with the project managers.

Write README files for each folder where you have stored your data.

Guidelines about data organization

Identify in your project which files are raw data, intermediate files, and result files. Then make a folder to store each type of files. Example of type of files:

Raw Files: Files generated by dedicated technologies, example: RCPNL, FASTQ, FLOW. These files are not needed anymore after generating relevant intermediate files. These files after a point can be removed, especially if your project is about to conclude and you have generated relevant intermediate files.

First milestone intermediate files: Files directly generated after processing Raw files. Those are used for subsequent analysis. Example: OME.tif, BAM, CSV tables. Particularly those files will replace the need to use Raw files.

Milestone intermediate files: Files that after heavy computation (several days), or a super-long manual data curation are used for relevant subsequent analysis. These files are mainly produced using the first milestone intermediate file. Remove other intermediate files that are not milestones.

Result Files: Published results, key visualizations for presentations, data summaries to share with collaborators.

project_name/

├── README.txt

├── notebooks/ # Jupyter or R notebooks

│ └── analysis.ipynb

├── raw_data/ # Raw files (temporary, can be deleted later)

│ ├── sample1.fastq

│ ├── sample2.fastq

│ ├── experiment1.rcpnl

│ └── flowdata.fcs

├── intermediate_data/

│ ├── first_milestone/ # direct output from raw files

│ │ ├── sample1_ome.tif

│ │ ├── sample2_ome.tif

│ │ ├── alignments.bam

│ │ └── expression_matrix.csv

│ └── milestone/ # Heavy computation or manual curation

│ ├── curated_annotations.csv

│ ├── dimensionality_reduction_results.h5ad

│ └── spatial_features.parquet

├── results/ # Final results, visualizations, summary tables

│ ├── figures/

│ │ ├── fig1_UMAP.png

│ │ └── fig2_survival_curve.pdf

│ ├── tables/

│ │ └── final_summary_table.xlsx

│ └── presentations/

│ └── group_meeting_results.pptx

└── scripts/ # Scripts for preprocessing, analysis, visualization

├── preprocess_raw.py

├── analysis_pipeline.R

└── plot_results.py

Identify redundant files:

Typical redundant files: Result1, Result_final, Result_the_one. Keep the most updated, remove all other versions. Use dates to differentiate which version is the most updated, example: Results_2811204. Save your scripts in https://github.com/farkkilab to re-generate any of the other results versions if needed.

Remove all intermediate files which can be easily re-generated using your scripts.

Remove data from experiments that did not work and are probably will never be missed, specially if you already have result files.

Add a README in each folder to describe what each file is, if even you can not remember what that file is, then maybe it is not needed anymore, delete it!

Organize and Standardize File Naming

Implement a consistent naming convention including:

Project name, experiment ID, and file type (e.g., raw, intermediate/Spatial_analysis, intermediate/Quantification, intermediate/Cell_visualization, others, Results/Microscopy_screenshots).

Date format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD) and versioning for easy identification.

Give a meaningful long name to your files. Cell_quantification_2pixels_myleoid_cells_28112024.csv
Cell_quantification_2pixels_lymphoid_cells_28112024.csv
